title: Boat Rental in Turquoise Coast: 2026 Guide & Costs
html:
Boat rental in the Turquoise Coast offers a gateway to the crystalline waters of the Mediterranean, with prices starting from around 250 EUR for a half-day small motorboat from Kas. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners for a seamless booking experience. The region's sheltered bays and ancient ruins make it a premier cruising ground.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in the Turquoise Coast?

Rental costs vary widely, but expect to pay from 250 EUR for a small motorboat half-day to 3,500 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht per day. You can find boat rental Boat Rental options on BoatHire24, where prices are transparent and inclusive of basic insurance. Budget extra for fuel if you take the helm yourself.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in the Turquoise Coast?

For most motorboats under 15hp and sailing boats under 30m², no licence is required if you stick to day trips. Larger vessels often require an ICC or equivalent. Always check with the operator for exact requirements, as local regulations may vary.

A local tip: ask the marina master at Ciftlik Bay for the best spot to anchor for the night. The water is glassy calm after 6 pm.

Where to set off in the Turquoise Coast

Kas Harbour is the most popular departure point, with dozens of rental boats available daily. Other excellent marinas include Fethiye, Gocek, and Kalkan. The islands of Kekova and the sunken city are a short cruise away. Demre and Olympos offer alternative starting points.

  • Kas Harbour, sheltered, with fuel and chandlery.
  • Fethiye Marina, modern facilities, access to Butterfly Valley.
  • Gocek Yacht Marina, luxury base for gulet charters.
  • Kalkan Yacht Harbour, quiet, with good restaurants.
  • Kekova, no marina, but anchorage for ruins.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size, budget, and desire for a skipper. Below are common options available through BoatHire24.

  1. Small motorboat (Azimut 40), perfect for two couples, half-day from 250 EUR, no licence needed.
  2. RIB tender, ideal for up to 6 guests, zips between coves, from 350 EUR full day.
  3. Sailing yacht (Sun Odyssey 440), four cabins, great for overnight trips, from 600 EUR per day.
  4. Catamaran (Lagoon 450), spacious for 8, stable for families, from 1,000 EUR per day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (Prestige 500), with crew, for 8 guests, from 2,500 EUR per day. See the Prestige 500 listing for exact specs.

Cost breakdown for boat rental in Turquoise Coast

  • Shared tour (8 guests, 4 hours): from 80 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 guests): from 250 EUR.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 guests): from 1,200 EUR.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (8 guests): from 2,500 EUR.

At a glance

  • Price band: 250, 3,500 EUR per day.
  • Peak months: July, August, September.
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day.
  • Guest capacity: up to 8.
  • Currency: EUR (some operators accept Turkish Lira).

Questions about boat rental in the Turquoise Coast

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, many small motorboats and RIBs are self-drive, but you must have some experience and the operator may require proof.

What is the best time of year for boating in the Turquoise Coast?

The high season runs from June to September, when the meltemi wind is moderate and water temperatures are 26°C.

Are there hidden costs like fuel or cleaning fees?

Most BoatHire24 listings state what is included: fuel is often extra unless specified; cleaning fees are rare but check the terms.

Do I need to book in advance for 2026?

Peak summer weekends fill quickly, so booking at least a month ahead is wise. BoatHire24 allows instant confirmation for many boats.

Boat rental in the Turquoise Coast is an unforgettable way to explore ancient Lycian coastline. Whether you choose a self-drive motorboat or a crewed yacht, BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare and book. If you own a boat, you can also list your vessel to earn income from travellers worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in the Turquoise Coast and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
